purpose.summary: Execution-level decomposition of parent backlog tasks into narrower bounded slices for agent orchestration.
rules: Keep parent tasks in tasks.json as orchestration-level authority.
Use this file for narrow execution slices only.
Each slice should target one clean output boundary.
If a parent task is blocked or moved to another PRD track, its slices stay blocked here as well.

phases.title: Phase A. Cleanup / Reset to Valid Core
Phase B. PRD Implementation on Clean Baseline
Phase C. Post-Implementation Cleanup and Authority Hardening
Phase D. LLM Gateway Foundation and Controlled Adoption
Phase E. PRD Alignment and Runtime Hardening
Phase F. Operator Interaction Layer Execution Backlog

slices.description: Produce a code-backed valid-core inventory note covering src/, deploy/, runtime entrypoints, and planning authority surfaces.
acceptance_focus: Inventory clearly separates keep, keep_with_refactor, quarantine_disable, and delete_rewrite.
Every retained area is justified against the PRD and current code.
slices.notes: Clean split because it only establishes the inventory artifact.

slices.description: Write the reset-boundary note that freezes current Stage6/web/tgbot as legacy and defines the retained baseline.
acceptance_focus: Note explicitly rejects old Stage6/web/tgbot as target architecture.
Retained baseline is limited to reusable substrate and runtime shell.
slices.notes: Clean split because the policy note depends on the inventory but is a separate output.

slices.description: Inventory planning and runbook documents that still present Stage6/web/tgbot as active direction.
acceptance_focus: Every active planning entrypoint is classified as keep, archive, or deprecate.
Legacy authority surfaces are explicitly identified.
slices.notes: Clean split because this is doc discovery only. Inventory note added at docs/planning/CLEANUP-002-A_PLANNING_AND_RUNBOOK_INVENTORY_2026-04-02.md.

slices.description: Update docs/planning authority chain so the PRD and reset boundary become the active planning entrypoints.
acceptance_focus: Planning README points to clean-slate materials.
No active doc claims the old Stage6/web/tgbot path is current.
slices.notes: Clean split because index/routing updates are distinct from discovery. Planning README now points to PRD, reset boundary, and backlog authority.

slices.description: Inventory Stage6-specific models and interfaces in TgAssistant.Core and mark exact removal, relocation, or legacy-gate targets.
acceptance_focus: Exact list of Stage6 contracts in active core namespaces is captured.
Each contract has a chosen treatment path.
slices.notes: Clean split because this is contract classification, not code mutation. Inventory note added at docs/planning/CLEANUP-003-A_CORE_CONTRACT_INVENTORY_2026-04-02.md.

slices.description: Apply the shared-model shrink: remove, relocate, or clearly legacy-gate Stage6/current-state/profile/strategy/draft/review/case contracts.
acceptance_focus: Active core namespaces only expose substrate and minimal operational primitives.
Legacy contracts are no longer presented as active foundation APIs.
slices.notes: Clean split because this is the code mutation step after classification. Legacy Stage6/current-state/profile/strategy/draft/review/case contracts moved behind TgAssistant.Core.Legacy namespaces, IRepositories.cs was split to keep legacy repositories out of the active core surface, and dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Create a schema inventory that labels preserved substrate tables, frozen legacy tables, and replace-on-rebuild tables.
acceptance_focus: Every active table family has a lifecycle label.
Legacy Stage6/domain truth tables are not treated as canonical target storage.
slices.notes: Clean split because it defines storage policy before repository changes. Inventory note added at docs/planning/CLEANUP-004-A_SCHEMA_INVENTORY_2026-04-02.md.

slices.description: Refit repository registrations and DbContext boundary markers so frozen Stage6 repositories are no longer presented as active architecture.
acceptance_focus: Repository wiring distinguishes preserved substrate from frozen legacy.
Migration policy for new PRD storage is explicit.
slices.notes: Clean split because repository boundary work should follow schema classification. Repository registrations now separate active baseline repositories from legacy domain/Stage6 repositories, and TgAssistantDbContext explicitly marks frozen legacy mappings.

slices.description: Inventory runtime roles, hosted-service registrations, and smoke commands that currently normalize Stage6/web/tgbot as baseline runtime.
acceptance_focus: Every legacy runtime role and smoke entrypoint is mapped.
Preserved substrate/runtime shell roles are separately identified.
slices.notes: Clean split because inventory precedes composition changes. Runtime role, hosted-service, and smoke surfaces are classified in docs/planning/CLEANUP-005-A_RUNTIME_ROLE_AND_SMOKE_INVENTORY_2026-04-02.md.

slices.description: Refit Program.cs and startup wiring so preserved substrate roles are active baseline and Stage6/web/bot roles are not.
acceptance_focus: Host composition no longer assumes Stage6/web/bot as default runtime baseline.
Active role set is limited to preserved substrate and reusable shell.
slices.notes: Clean split because this is the host mutation step. Active runtime combinations now exclude stage6/web roles, ops no longer registers TelegramBotHostedService in the baseline host composition, and dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Rename or narrow smoke and runtime documentation so preserved substrate verification is clearly separated from legacy diagnostics.
acceptance_focus: Smoke names and docs no longer suggest old Stage6/web/tgbot is active baseline.
Preserved verification paths are easy to identify.
slices.notes: Clean split because naming and docs should trail the actual runtime wiring change. Smoke/help text now separates preserved verification entrypoints from legacy diagnostic-only entrypoints, launch step labels no longer use web-prefixed names, and runtime docs mark retired Stage6 paths as legacy-only.

slices.description: Gate or remove active Stage6 registrations and helper command entrypoints from the cleaned runtime composition.
acceptance_focus: No active startup path registers the current Stage6 engine as baseline behavior.
Legacy Stage6 helper commands are not part of the clean runtime contract.
slices.notes: Clean split because this isolates the main legacy engine. Program.cs now rejects legacy Stage6 execution/auto-case/light-ab CLI entrypoints at startup, those entrypoints were removed from the active --list-smokes contract, and DomainRegistrationExtensions no longer registers the matching Stage6 verification/command services in the active composition root.

slices.description: Apply explicit legacy markings to any retained Stage6 code left for reference or temporary diagnostics.
acceptance_focus: Retained Stage6 code cannot be confused with PRD Stage 6/7/8.
Legacy-only intent is visible in code and notes.
slices.notes: Clean split because it is a labeling/containment step after runtime isolation. Added legacy-only headers across src/TgAssistant.Intelligence/Stage6 and a folder boundary README to prevent confusion with PRD Stage 6/7/8.

slices.description: Quarantine or disable the current web operator surface from active runtime composition.
acceptance_focus: Current web routes are not presented as target operator workflow.
Any retained shell is diagnostic-only.
slices.notes: Clean split because web isolation is independent from bot isolation. Legacy web services now register only for explicit diagnostic flags, launch verification no longer depends on the web surface, the retained WebRuntimeHostedService is labeled diagnostic-only, and dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Quarantine or disable the current Telegram bot operator workflow from active runtime composition.
acceptance_focus: Current Telegram bot flow is not presented as target operator workflow.
Any retained bot shell is clearly temporary or diagnostic-only.
slices.notes: Clean split because bot isolation has a different code path and validation surface. Legacy bot command/chat services now register only for explicit --bot-smoke diagnostics, ops runtime no longer requires Telegram bot credentials, and TelegramBotHostedService is marked as a retained legacy diagnostic shell.

slices.description: Add explicit diagnostic-only labeling wherever a temporary web or bot shell remains reachable.
acceptance_focus: Temporary shells cannot be mistaken for product baseline.
Legacy Stage6 queue/case/artifact flow is visibly isolated.
slices.notes: Clean split because it is a post-isolation containment task. Program.cs now labels legacy bot/web smoke paths as diagnostic-only in list output and exit logs.

slices.description: Classify current MCP tool catalog into reusable shell tools versus Stage6-specific legacy tools.
acceptance_focus: Reusable health, auth, and transport shell responsibilities are isolated.
Stage6 case/artifact-centric tools are explicitly identified for removal or gating.
slices.notes: Clean split because tool classification should precede code changes. Inventory note added at docs/planning/CLEANUP-008-A_MCP_TOOL_CATALOG_INVENTORY_2026-04-02.md.

slices.description: Strip or legacy-gate Stage6-specific MCP tools while preserving the reusable MCP transport/auth shell.
acceptance_focus: Primary MCP contract is no longer Stage6-centric.
Build remains healthy after tool catalog cleanup.
slices.notes: Clean split because it applies the tool classification. Stage6 MCP tool registrations removed from src/TgAssistant.Mcp/index.ts; reusable shell tools retained.

slices.description: Update docker-compose and deploy assumptions so runtime shell survives without web/bot/Stage6 operator flow as baseline.
acceptance_focus: Compose and deploy docs reflect preserved shell only.
No deploy default implies old operator flow is still accepted baseline.
slices.notes: Clean split because infra defaults should trail MCP/runtime cleanup. Default docker-compose app baseline no longer injects web/bot/Stage6 env; .env.example and deploy docs mark those paths as legacy diagnostic-only or preview-only.

slices.description: Run cleanup review across docs, shared models, schema boundaries, runtime wiring, Stage6 isolation, web/bot isolation, and MCP/deploy shell.
acceptance_focus: Review note records blocking findings or explicit approval.
Any remaining legacy dependency is called out.
slices.notes: Clean split because this is the audit pass itself. Review note added at docs/planning/CLEANUP-009-A_CLEANUP_REVIEW_2026-04-02.md with blocking findings: active composition root and launch readiness still depend on legacy Stage6 services.

slices.description: Apply cleanup review fixes or record explicit approval to open Phase B implementation.
acceptance_focus: Phase B start condition is explicit.
Unresolved cleanup blockers cannot leak into implementation.
slices.notes: Clean split because gate resolution is separate from the review run. Stage6 verification services moved behind includeLegacyStage6Diagnostics; preserved launch bundle narrowed to foundation, stage5, and budget-visibility; cleanup approval granted.

slices.description: Write the target architecture note mapping PRD terms to concrete modules, storage boundaries, runtime flows, and rollout cut lines.
acceptance_focus: Stage 5/6/7/8 responsibilities map cleanly to modules.
No hidden dependency on legacy Stage6/web/tgbot remains.
slices.notes: Clean split because this is the implementation authority artifact. Architecture note added at docs/planning/IMPLEMENT-001-A_TARGET_ARCHITECTURE_NOTE_2026-04-02.md.

slices.description: Publish a workstream and dependency map derived from the architecture note for data platform, substrate refit, control plane, bootstrap, durable formation, crystallization, and pilot.
acceptance_focus: Execution order is explicit enough for agent orchestration.
No major module is left without an owning workstream.
slices.notes: Clean split because dependency mapping is a separate planning deliverable. Workstream map added at docs/planning/IMPLEMENT-001-B_WORKSTREAM_AND_DEPENDENCY_MAP_2026-04-02.md.

slices.description: Add the person-first identity schema: Person, operator linkage, candidate identity state, and relationship-edge anchors.
acceptance_focus: Person and identity state objects exist independently of frozen Stage6 truth tables.
Identity lookups are queryable by scope and source binding.
slices.notes: Clean split because identity schema is foundational and independent. Added migration 0030_person_identity_schema.sql with persons, person_operator_links, person_identity_bindings, candidate_identity_states, and relationship_edge_anchors; mapped the new active tables in TgAssistantDbContext/DbRows; dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Add SourceObject and EvidenceItem schema with provenance, source refs, and linkage to the person-first model.
acceptance_focus: Source and evidence records support corpus-first rebuildability.
Evidence can be queried by person, source, and provenance.
slices.notes: Clean split because evidence storage can be validated separately from run ledgers. Added migration 0031_source_object_and_evidence_schema.sql with source_objects, evidence_items, and evidence_item_person_links; mapped the new active tables in TgAssistantDbContext/DbRows with indexes for source, provenance, and person-scope evidence lookups; dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Add ModelPassRun, NormalizationRun, and durable-object metadata schema for auditability and later promotion state.
acceptance_focus: Run ledgers and durable metadata exist with stable indexes.
Later passes can attach run and promotion references cleanly.
slices.notes: Clean split because run audit schema is a separate bounded concern. Added migration 0032_model_pass_and_durable_metadata_schema.sql with model_pass_runs, normalization_runs, durable_object_metadata, and durable_object_evidence_links; mapped the new active tables in TgAssistantDbContext/DbRows with indexes for run-id, target, person-scope, and evidence-ref lookups; dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Refit realtime ingest to write new source, evidence, and provenance records against the person-first schema.
acceptance_focus: Realtime pipeline populates the new substrate objects.
No durable person conclusions are emitted from Stage 5.
slices.notes: Clean split because realtime ingest is a separate pipeline boundary. BatchWorkerService now resolves persisted realtime messages after SaveBatchAsync and upserts source_objects/evidence_items/evidence_item_person_links through RealtimeMessageSubstrateRepository; writes remain substrate-only and dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Refit archive import and session slicing to populate the new source/evidence/provenance model.
acceptance_focus: Archive replay populates the new substrate consistently.
Session slicing remains rebuildable and corpus-first.
slices.notes: Clean split because archive flow has different write path and replay semantics. ArchiveImportWorkerService now resolves persisted archive messages after SaveBatchAsync and upserts source_objects/evidence_items through ArchiveMessageSubstrateRepository, while ChatSessionRepository atomically upserts session-boundary source/evidence records alongside chat_sessions writes; changes remain substrate-only and dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Refit media enrichment to write media-derived evidence and provenance into the new substrate model.
acceptance_focus: Media-derived evidence uses the same source and provenance model.
No legacy Stage6 artifact writes remain in Stage 5 media flow.
slices.notes: Clean split because media processing is an independent extension path. MessageRepository now writes canonical media_enrichment and voice_paralinguistics evidence_items inside the existing media-processing persistence boundary, reusing source_objects provenance and mirroring person links from the source message evidence; dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Run deterministic rebuild verification across realtime, archive, and media paths against the new substrate objects.
acceptance_focus: Replay twice yields stable counts and identities.
Stage 5 remains a rebuildable substrate only.
slices.notes: Clean split because deterministic verification should gate pipeline refit completion. --stage5-smoke now runs Stage5SubstrateDeterminismVerificationService, which replays synthetic realtime, archive, session, and media writes twice against the active substrate tables and fails on count or identity drift or any non-substrate writes under the synthetic scope; dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Define and implement the pass envelope schema with explicit scope, source refs, truth summary, conflicts, unknowns, and result statuses.
acceptance_focus: Pass envelope is versioned and storage-safe.
Statuses result_ready, need_more_data, need_operator_clarification, and blocked_invalid_input are supported.
slices.notes: Clean split because envelope contract precedes normalization. Added typed ModelPassEnvelope models plus ModelPassEnvelopeRepository/validator/storage codec over model_pass_runs, introduced additive 0033_model_pass_envelope_schema.sql columns for explicit scope/source_refs/truth_summary/conflicts/unknowns/result_status, and added --pass-envelope-smoke to validate a result_ready success path plus a blocked_invalid_input failure path without runtime secrets; dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Implement the normalization layer that converts model output into typed fact, inference, hypothesis, and conflict candidates.
acceptance_focus: Raw model prose cannot write directly into durable state.
Unsafe or malformed output is downgraded or blocked.
slices.notes: Clean split because the normalizer is a distinct contract boundary. Added typed ModelNormalization models plus ModelOutputNormalizer to parse only whitelisted JSON candidate arrays into fact/inference/hypothesis/conflict candidates, downgrade partially malformed result_ready payloads to need_operator_clarification, and block raw prose or invalid shapes before any durable write path; added --normalization-smoke to validate result_ready, downgrade, and blocked_invalid_input paths in-memory, and dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Persist normalization and pass audit records and add verification for normal, degraded, clarification, and blocked outcomes.
acceptance_focus: Every pass outcome leaves audit evidence.
Storage behavior is consistent across allowed result states.
slices.notes: Clean split because audit persistence and test coverage are a separate completion step. Added ModelPassAuditService and ModelPassAuditStore to normalize and atomically upsert model_pass_runs plus normalization_runs in one DbContext save, extended normalization_runs with issues_json audit storage, and added --model-pass-audit-smoke to verify persisted result_ready, need_more_data, need_operator_clarification, and blocked_invalid_input outcomes; dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln plus pass-envelope, normalization, and model-pass-audit smokes passed.

slices.description: Implement Stage 6 bootstrap graph initialization and tracked-person attachment over the prepared corpus.
acceptance_focus: Bootstrap can anchor one tracked person or scope to a graph seed.
Outputs remain non-durable.
slices.notes: Clean split because graph init is the first bounded bootstrap behavior. Added Stage6BootstrapService plus Stage6BootstrapRepository to resolve one tracked_person against active person_operator_links and evidence-backed substrate coverage, persist non-durable bootstrap_graph_nodes/bootstrap_graph_edges idempotently by scope natural keys, and record bootstrap audit state through model_pass_runs plus normalization_runs; added --stage6-bootstrap-smoke to validate ready and need_more_data paths and rerun identity stability, and dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Implement linked-person discovery plus candidate identity and mention-state differentiation in bootstrap outputs.
acceptance_focus: Linked person, candidate identity, and mention are stored distinctly.
Discovery outputs are idempotent across reruns.
slices.notes: Clean split because identity discovery should be validated separately from graph init. Stage6BootstrapRepository now writes non-durable bootstrap_discovery_outputs keyed by tracked-person scope natural identity, differentiating linked_person rows from unresolved candidate_identity and mention outputs discovered from person-linked evidence and candidate_identity_states; Stage6BootstrapService returns discovery outputs alongside graph init, and --stage6-bootstrap-smoke now verifies distinct discovery types plus stable discovery IDs across reruns.

slices.description: Implement bootstrap ambiguity, contradiction, and slice outputs without durable promotion.
acceptance_focus: Bootstrap outputs expose ambiguity and contradiction pools explicitly.
No reviewed dossier/profile semantics leak into bootstrap.
slices.notes: Clean split because ambiguity/contradiction pools are a separate non-durable output family. Stage6BootstrapRepository now writes non-durable bootstrap_pool_outputs for ambiguity_pool, contradiction_pool, and bootstrap_slice keyed by tracked-person scope natural identity; Stage6BootstrapService returns those outputs alongside graph/discovery results, and --stage6-bootstrap-smoke verifies all three families plus stable rerun IDs without any durable dossier/profile writes.

slices.description: Implement durable dossier and profile formation from normalized candidates and bootstrap outputs.
acceptance_focus: Profile versus dossier is explicit in durable storage.
Evidence refs, freshness, and confidence are persisted.
slices.notes: Clean split because dossier/profile formation is one durable object family. Added separate durable_dossiers and durable_profiles tables backed by durable_object_metadata/evidence_links, plus Stage7DossierProfileFormationService to form dossier/profile outputs from stage6 bootstrap + normalized candidates with persisted confidence, freshness, coverage, and supporting evidence refs; added --stage7-dossier-profile-smoke to verify materialization, rerun idempotency, and non-ready bootstrap failure semantics, and dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Implement durable pair-dynamics formation as a first-class object family with revisions and contradiction markers.
acceptance_focus: Pair dynamics are not embedded as summary strings.
Revision behavior preserves confidence and freshness history.
slices.notes: Clean split because pair dynamics has its own storage and rules. Added durable_pair_dynamics plus durable_pair_dynamics_revisions as a first-class Stage 7 family backed by shared durable_object_metadata/evidence_links, with Stage7PairDynamicsFormationService/Repository materializing structured pair dimensions, contradiction markers, and family-local revision history keyed by revision hash; added --stage7-pair-dynamics-smoke to verify materialization, stable identity on identical rerun, new revision creation on changed pair pressure, and no durable output for non-ready bootstrap results.

slices.description: Implement durable events, timeline episodes, and story arcs as separate object families with boundary and closure metadata.
acceptance_focus: Timeline uses episodes and story arcs as end-state objects.
Boundary confidence and closure state are explicit.
slices.notes: Clean split because timeline objects are independent from dossier and pair dynamics. Added explicit Stage7 timeline/event/story-arc durable families with dedicated storage tables, boundary_confidence and closure_state on each object, and Stage7TimelineFormationService/Repository materializing one durable event, one timeline episode, and one story arc from ready bootstrap output via shared durable metadata/evidence links; added --stage7-timeline-smoke to verify materialization, stable rerun identity, and no durable outputs for non-ready bootstrap results.

slices.description: Implement durable revision and idempotent rerun behavior across all Stage 7 object families.
acceptance_focus: Reruns create revisions instead of duplicate durable rows.
Object-family-specific revision rules are consistent.
slices.notes: Clean split because revision behavior should be validated after object families exist. Added current_revision_number/current_revision_hash plus revision history storage for dossier/profile and timeline families, reused a shared Stage7 revision hash helper across repositories, and extended the stage7 dossier/profile, pair-dynamics, and timeline smokes to verify same-input reruns reuse stable durable IDs and revision IDs while changed-input reruns create new revisions on the same durable objects; dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln and all three stage7 family smokes passed.

slices.description: Implement the persistent recompute queue with dedupe, retry, and smallest-sufficient-scope execution.
acceptance_focus: Scoped recompute can run independently of global rebuilds.
Queue behavior is idempotent and retry-safe.
slices.notes: Clean split because queue infrastructure is foundational to the implemented Stage8 scoped-recompute baseline. Added persistent stage8_recompute_queue_items storage plus Stage8RecomputeQueueRepository/Service for deduped enqueue, lease, complete, and retry-safe reschedule behavior, with smallest-sufficient-scope execution that runs stage6_bootstrap directly or the requested stage7 family through bootstrap as upstream context; --stage8-recompute-smoke validates dedupe, scoped dossier/profile execution, and transient-failure retry/reschedule, and dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln passed.

slices.description: Implement trigger ingestion for clarification answers, edits, deletes, and high-signal data changes.
acceptance_focus: Each trigger class maps to a targeted rerun request.
No trigger forces a global rebuild by default.
slices.notes: Implemented and pushed: DomainReviewEvent-based Stage8 trigger ingestion now enqueues scoped recompute requests via Stage8RecomputeTriggerService and repository wiring.

slices.description: Implement promotion and clarification gates on the new truth-layer model for Stage8 scoped-recompute outcomes.
acceptance_focus: Promotion outcomes are explicit and auditable.
Clarification-gated scopes do not over-promote durable knowledge.
slices.notes: Implemented and pushed: added Stage8 truth-layer outcome gating via Stage8OutcomeGateRepository and wired it into Stage8RecomputeQueueService so scoped recompute outcomes now mark durable_object_metadata promotion_state/truth_layer as promoted, promotion_blocked, or clarification_blocked with auditable stage8_gate metadata; extended --stage8-recompute-smoke coverage for clarification-gated outcomes and verified with dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln.

slices.description: Prepare interface-track boundary note for future person-root web/tg surfaces on top of the clean model.
acceptance_focus: Future interface track references the clean model only.
No legacy Stage6 operator pages are reused by assumption.
slices.notes: Clean split because governance is separate from trigger/queue mechanics.

slices.description: Define future programmatic read contracts for dossier, profile, pair, timeline, clarification, and review objects.
acceptance_focus: Future UI track has stable read contracts to build against.
Read contracts are rooted in the clean model, not legacy case/chat pages.
slices.notes: Blocked because interface work is outside the current backend-core track.

slices.description: Implement metrics and rollups for pass families, normalization outcomes, promotion blocks, contradiction load, clarification load, recompute depth, and model spend.
acceptance_focus: Core pipeline metrics are queryable by scope and run family.
Spend and queue depth are visible for governance.
slices.notes: Clean split because metrics can ship independently of control-state enforcement.

slices.description: Implement defect taxonomy persistence and escalation rules for ingestion, data, identity, model, normalization, control-plane, cost, and semantic-drift failures.
acceptance_focus: Defects are stored with class, severity, and scope impact.
Escalation rules exist for the major failure classes.
slices.notes: Implemented and pushed: added runtime_defects taxonomy persistence (migration 0043 + DbContext mapping + RuntimeDefectRepository) with explicit classes for ingestion/data/identity/model/normalization/control_plane/cost/semantic_drift and escalation policy mapping to observe/ticket/review_only/budget_protected/safe_mode; wired Stage8RecomputeQueueService to record and escalate defects for execution failures and gated outcomes, and extended --stage8-recompute-smoke assertions for defect persistence paths.

slices.description: Implement runtime control states and enforcement points such as normal, degraded, review_only, budget_protected, promotion_blocked, and safe_mode.
acceptance_focus: Runtime states are both visible and enforceable.
Control states integrate with pass execution and promotion paths.
slices.notes: Implemented and pushed: added runtime_control_states persistence (migration 0044 + repository/service), defect-driven control-state evaluation with precedence across normal/degraded/review_only/budget_protected/promotion_blocked/safe_mode, and Stage8 enforcement points that defer execution under safe_mode/budget_protected/degraded and force promotion_blocked under review_only/promotion_blocked; stage8 gate metadata now records runtime control context.

slices.description: Implement a resumable pilot rebuild and backfill flow from preserved substrate-only data into the clean model.
acceptance_focus: Pilot rebuild resumes cleanly after interruption.
Counts and object identities remain stable across restart.
slices.notes: Clean split because rebuild mechanics are independent from docs and review.

slices.description: Update core runbooks for pilot rollout, rebuild, rollback, and verification against the clean architecture.
acceptance_focus: Runbooks describe clean-architecture pilot behavior, not legacy Stage6/web/tgbot flows.
Commands and service names match the current codebase.
slices.notes: Clean split because operational docs should follow a working pilot flow.

slices.description: Run the pre-rollout audit and record go/no-go status for pilot widening.
acceptance_focus: Pre-rollout review note exists with findings or approval.
Pilot widening cannot proceed while blocking findings remain open.
slices.notes: Clean split because review gate should stay independent from implementation changes.

slices.description: Add per-pass iteration, token, and cost budgets for Stage 6/7/8 runs and persist the budget envelope in run audit state.
acceptance_focus: Every relevant pass family has explicit loop and spend budgets.
Budget envelopes are auditable by run and scope.
slices.notes: Completed by adding typed model-pass budget envelopes for Stage6 bootstrap and Stage7 durable passes, persisting them in model_pass_runs metrics/input audit payloads, and aligning audit/storage/launch smokes so run budgets remain auditable by run and scope.

slices.description: Enforce retrieval-loop termination and escalation when repeated need_more_data responses exceed configured pass budgets.
acceptance_focus: Runs terminate into explicit blocked or escalated outcomes instead of looping indefinitely.
Loop termination integrates with defects and runtime control states.
slices.notes: Completed by counting consecutive need_more_data outcomes per scope/stage/pass-family in the audit store, escalating exhausted loops into auditable need_operator_clarification outcomes with persisted loop_budget_exhausted signals, emitting runtime normalization defects for control-state evaluation, and updating launch/audit smokes to cover the terminal guard path.

slices.description: Persist unresolved clarification branches with branch-local blocking semantics instead of whole-graph blocking.
acceptance_focus: Blocked graph branches are explicit and queryable.
Blocked state is scoped to affected branches only.
slices.notes: Completed by adding clarification_branch_states persistence plus EF mappings, singleton repository wiring, and audit-path integration that opens branch-scoped clarification blocks on need_operator_clarification, keeps unrelated blocked outcomes from clearing them, resolves the branch on result_ready, and exposes query methods by scope and branch family.

slices.description: Allow Stage 7 and Stage 8 to continue on unaffected graph branches while blocked branches remain unresolved.
acceptance_focus: Unaffected dossier, pair, and timeline branches continue progressing safely.
Affected branches remain blocked and auditable until clarification arrives.
slices.notes: Completed by teaching Stage8RecomputeQueueService to query clarification_branch_states before execution, short-circuit only the matching blocked target family into an auditable need_operator_clarification completion with clarification gating preserved, and extending --stage8-recompute-smoke to prove a blocked timeline branch stays blocked while dossier/profile work in the same scope continues.

slices.description: Implement reversible identity-merge history with merge confidence tiers and preserved before/after state.
acceptance_focus: Merges and splits can be reversed without losing prior state.
Weak merges are clearly tiered and review-gated.
slices.notes: Implemented active identity merge history via 0046_identity_merge_histories.sql plus IdentityMergeRepository over persons, person_identity_bindings, person_operator_links, candidate_identity_states, and relationship_edge_anchors. Merge requests now persist before/after snapshots, weak or cross-type merges remain pending_review unless explicitly approved, applied merges rewrite the active identity graph transactionally, and ReverseAsync restores the preserved pre-merge state without pulling recompute logic into this slice.

slices.description: Implement bounded invalidation and recompute after merge or split corrections so identity mistakes do not require global reruns by default.
acceptance_focus: Corrections invalidate only affected graph and durable branches by default.
Global rerun remains exceptional rather than normal recovery.
slices.notes: Completed by extending IdentityMergeRepository with bounded correction plans that invalidate only durable_object_metadata rows tied to the affected owner/related person ids, persist recompute_plan_json plus recompute_enqueued_at_utc on identity_merge_histories, and enqueue scoped Stage8 dossier/profile, pair-dynamics, and timeline reruns for the touched persons instead of any global rerun.

slices.description: Create the canonical dossier-field registry and alias map for durable field families.

slices.notes: Done on 2026-04-04: added active Stage8 related-conflict reevaluation after successful recompute gating via Stage8RelatedConflictRepository, which reconciles target-family durable contradiction markers into `domain_conflict_records`, auto-resolves no-longer-relevant records with explicit `auto_closed_after_recompute_no_remaining_contradictions` reason plus audit events, and keeps remaining/new conflicts linked to refreshed durable metadata/object keys without pulling OPINT-003-C/D UI lifecycle work into scope. Verified with `dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln` and `dotnet run --project src/TgAssistant.Host/TgAssistant.Host.csproj -- --stage8-related-conflict-smoke`.

slices.description: Persist and project recompute result lifecycle states (running/done/failed/clarification_blocked) for UI feedback contracts.
acceptance_focus: UI-facing status projection is deterministic and queryable.
Failure/degraded states are surfaced, not hidden behind success-only paths.
slices.notes: Done on 2026-04-04: operator resolution actions now persist recompute lifecycle summary columns and project action/target UI feedback states (`running`/`done`/`failed`/`clarification_blocked`) from Stage8 queue state, with deduped queue reuse still refreshing linked action rows via accepted audit details. Verified with `dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln` and `dotnet run --project src/TgAssistant.Host/TgAssistant.Host.csproj -- --resolution-recompute-contract-smoke`.

slices.description: Execute bounded seeded-scope end-to-end validation: action -> recompute -> related-conflict transition -> feedback projection artifact.
acceptance_focus: Recompute-after-resolution loop is evidence-backed on bounded runtime.
Execution artifacts are sufficient for P0 surface implementation runs.
slices.notes: Done on 2026-04-04: added bounded runtime validator entrypoint `--opint-003-d-validate` plus evidence note `docs/planning/OPINT-003-D_VALIDATION_2026-04-04.md`, then ran `dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln` and `dotnet run --project src/TgAssistant.Host/TgAssistant.Host.csproj -- --operator-schema-init --opint-003-d-validate --opint-003-d-validate-output=/home/codex/projects/TelegramAssistant/logs/opint-003-d-validation-report.json` against bounded scope `chat:885574984`. Validation proved the normal loop `action -> recompute -> related-conflict transition -> replayed lifecycle feedback` (`running -> done`, `created=1`, `resolved=1`, `domain_review_events=2`) and a degraded `clarification_blocked` lifecycle path with zero residual seed rows after cleanup. During validation a real runtime defect was confirmed and fixed in `Stage8RecomputeQueueRepository`: linked action lifecycle refresh was issuing `LIKE` against `jsonb` audit details, which failed during recompute completion on Postgres.

slices.description: Implement Telegram mode selection card and active tracked-person context state for resolution mode.
acceptance_focus: Telegram no longer defaults to free chat and requires mode/context selection.
Active tracked-person state is explicit and switchable.
slices.notes: Foundation for compact operator cockpit behavior.

slices.description: Render compact Telegram resolution cards with required metadata and bounded action controls.
acceptance_focus: Cards include type/summary/why/trust/status/evidence/updated-at.
Actions map directly to backend bounded contracts without legacy bot command reuse.
slices.notes: Done on 2026-04-04: Telegram resolution mode now renders compact card messages with required metadata fields (`type/summary/why/trust/status/evidence/updated-at`) and bounded action controls mapped to OPINT contracts (`approve/reject/defer/clarify/evidence/open-web`) via tokenized callbacks, without legacy bot command reuse. `approve` is wired end-to-end to bounded action submission while explanation-requiring actions remain explicitly deferred to OPINT-004-C. Verified with `dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln` and bounded Telegram smoke `dotnet run --project src/TgAssistant.Host -- --opint-004-a-smoke --runtime-role=ops`.

slices.description: Implement Telegram clarification submission flow for clarify/reject/defer with bounded follow-up input and structured payload persistence.
acceptance_focus: Clarify/reject/defer actions require bounded explanation input when configured by the action contract.
Submitted payload keeps operator/session/person/item scope and is auditable in persisted action data.
slices.notes: 2026-04-04: Telegram resolution workflow now captures bounded explanation input for clarify/reject/defer with pending-step session binding and structured clarification payload metadata (operator/session/person/item/action/scope). Build passes; runtime OPINT smoke requires a real DB connection string in this environment.

slices.description: Implement Telegram evidence preview and Open in Web handoff actions with bounded tracked-person/item context.
acceptance_focus: Evidence action shows compact bounded evidence summary without expanding Telegram into deep-analysis workspace.
Open in Web handoff includes stable bounded context (person/item/session) and avoids legacy web routes.
slices.notes: 2026-04-04: Telegram resolution `evidence` now renders compact bounded evidence preview (item/status/trust + top summarized evidence entries) and `open-web` now returns bounded handoff context (`tracked_person_id`, `scope_item_key`, `operator_session_id`, `active_mode`, API target, stable handoff token) without legacy web routes. Verified with `dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln`; runtime smoke command remains environment-blocked by placeholder `Database:ConnectionString`.

slices.description: Render Telegram recompute lifecycle feedback after submitted resolution actions.
acceptance_focus: Telegram displays running/done/failed/clarification-blocked recompute states for the acted item.
Post-action feedback shows unresolved/auto-resolved/newly-emerged related-conflict deltas for the same bounded scope.
slices.notes: 2026-04-04: Telegram resolution post-action output now includes recompute lifecycle feedback (`running`/`done`/`failed`/`clarification_blocked` + lifecycle/result/failure detail) and same-scope related-conflict deltas (`unresolved`/`auto-resolved`/`newly-emerged`) for approve/reject/defer/clarify submissions. Verified with `dotnet build TelegramAssistant.sln`; runtime smoke `dotnet run --project src/TgAssistant.Host/TgAssistant.Host.csproj -- --opint-004-a-smoke --runtime-role=ops` remains environment-blocked by placeholder `Database:ConnectionString`.

slices.description: Run bounded Telegram resolution pilot validation with authorization, action persistence, and recompute feedback checks.
acceptance_focus: A reproducible bounded Telegram smoke path validates authorize -> action -> persistence -> recompute feedback.
Unauthorized operator attempts are denied, audited, and visible in validation output.
slices.notes: 2026-04-04: Completed bounded Telegram resolution pilot validation by combining OPINT Telegram mode/authorization smoke (`--opint-004-a-smoke`), bounded action persistence authorization validation (`scripts/opint-002-resolution-contract-validate.sh`), and bounded recompute lifecycle validation (`--opint-003-d-validate`) with concrete artifacts in logs/opint-004-d-smoke-report.json and logs/opint-004-d-recompute-report.json; unauthorized attempts were denied/audited and authorize -> action -> persistence -> recompute feedback path is reproducible on scope `chat:885574984`.

slices.description: Implement web operator shell bootstrap and bounded Resolution route entry (loading/empty/error states included).
acceptance_focus: Operator can enter a dedicated Resolution route from web home/navigation without legacy Stage6 pages.
Resolution entry state handles loading, empty queue, and failure modes explicitly.
slices.notes: 2026-04-04: Added clean operator web shell bootstrap routes (`/operator`, `/operator/resolution`) in host runtime with explicit resolution entry loading/empty/error state rendering via `/operator/resolution/bootstrap`; home navigation now enters dedicated Resolution route and explicitly excludes legacy Stage6 web/queue/case pages.

slices.description: Implement web auth/session boundary for operator-resolution contracts.
acceptance_focus: Web resolution requests include trusted operator/session context instead of free-form unauthenticated payload construction.
Denied/expired session paths are deterministic and auditable for bounded operator flows.
slices.notes: 2026-04-04: Completed web auth/session boundary by enforcing token-gated trusted web operator identity/session resolution at `/api/operator/*` (request-supplied identity/session payload is overridden server-side), persisting server-issued web session state via header/cookie for bounded progression, and auditing deterministic denied/expired session paths (`auth_denied`, `session_not_found`, `session_expired`) through operator session audit events.

slices.description: Implement web resolution queue with priority sorting, filtering, unresolved counters, and latest-activity ordering.
acceptance_focus: Queue ordering follows critical/high/medium/low then activity recency.
Counts and filters are consistent with backend resolution projections for the active operator scope.
slices.notes: 2026-04-04: Implemented web resolution queue on `/operator/resolution` using clean `/api/operator/*` contracts with tracked-person scope selection, priority/status/type filtering, unresolved counters, and priority/activity sorting controls rendered from backend queue projections; legacy Stage6 web/queue/case pages remain excluded.

slices.description: Implement web resolution detail panel for selected queue item with why-it-matters/trust/provenance visibility.
acceptance_focus: Detail view exposes item type, summary, why-it-matters, trust factor, affected objects, and bounded status metadata.
Detail state transitions are deterministic across selection, refresh, and unavailable-item cases.
slices.notes: Detail-view checkpoint isolated from evidence drawer and mutation paths. Completed 2026-04-04: web resolution detail panel now supports deterministic selection, refresh, and unavailable-item handling with bounded item metadata and provenance visibility via /api/operator/resolution/detail/query.

slices.description: Implement evidence drawer/panel in web resolution detail flow.
acceptance_focus: Evidence drawer shows bounded summarized evidence with provenance and confidence cues for the selected item.
Evidence open/close/navigation keeps operator in the same resolution context without route reset.
slices.notes: Evidence read path separated from action-write paths. Completed 2026-04-04: /operator/resolution now provides an in-context evidence drawer with bounded summarized evidence, provenance/confidence cues, and open/close/navigation behavior that preserves selected queue/detail context without route reset.

slices.description: Implement bounded approve/reject/defer action submission from web resolution detail context.
acceptance_focus: Operator can submit approve/reject/defer from detail context without losing queue position.
Action submission persists bounded operator/session/item scope and returns deterministic status payload.
slices.notes: Single write scope for non-clarify action submission. Completed 2026-04-04: /operator/resolution detail now includes bounded approve/reject/defer submission controls that call /api/operator/resolution/actions in place, enforce required explanation for reject/defer, preserve queue/detail selection context during refresh, and render deterministic action + recompute status feedback from persisted operator action results.

slices.description: Implement clarification side panel/drawer follow-up flow and bounded clarify action submission.
acceptance_focus: Clarification drawer prompts for required follow-up fields and enforces configured explanation requirements.
Clarify submission keeps context in-place and stores structured follow-up payload for recompute.
slices.notes: Clarification workflow isolated from generic action buttons. Completed 2026-04-04: /operator/resolution now includes an in-context clarification drawer with required explanation + follow-up question/answer fields, structured clarify payload submission to /api/operator/resolution/actions, and context-preserving queue/detail refresh behavior after clarify acceptance.

slices.description: Render recompute progress/result feedback and updated related-conflict state in web resolution workflow.
acceptance_focus: UI reflects running/done/failure/clarification-blocked states after actions.
Auto-resolved, remaining, and newly-emerged conflicts are visible post-recompute.
slices.notes: Completes P0 recompute visibility requirement on web surface. Completed 2026-04-04: /operator/resolution now renders deterministic post-action recompute lifecycle feedback (running/done/failed/clarification_blocked) and projection-based related-conflict outcome deltas (auto-resolved, remaining, newly-emerged) from pre/post queue refresh while preserving in-place operator context.

slices.description: Run bounded web P0 resolution end-to-end validation and record execution artifact for queue/detail/evidence/clarification/recompute loop.
acceptance_focus: Web P0 resolution smoke path is reproducible and checks queue/detail/evidence/action/recompute transitions.
Validation artifact captures normal path, denied-action path, and recompute-failure/degraded path.
slices.notes: P0 surface validation checkpoint #2. Completed 2026-04-04: added bounded web validator script (`scripts/opint-005-web-resolution-validate.sh`), captured OPINT-005-F execution artifact at `logs/opint-005-f-web-validation-report.json` (queue/detail/evidence + clarify/approve/reject/defer + denied-action path), captured degraded recompute evidence at `logs/opint-005-f-recompute-degraded-report.json`, and recorded validation summary in `docs/planning/OPINT-005-F_VALIDATION_2026-04-04.md`.

slices.description: Define assistant response schema and rendering contract (Short Answer/What Is Known/What It Means/Recommendation/Trust%) with explicit truth labels.
acceptance_focus: Assistant output contract is deterministic, machine-checkable, and reviewable with bounded examples.
Truth-label and trust display requirements are explicit for implementation/testing across Telegram assistant mode.
slices.notes: Done 2026-04-04: published deterministic assistant response schema/rendering contract in docs/planning/OPINT-006-A_ASSISTANT_RESPONSE_CONTRACT_2026-04-04.md covering required sections, truth-label rules, trust rendering, bounded scope guardrails, and Open in Web handoff constraints for OPINT-006-B1/B2/C implementation and testing.

slices.description: Implement assistant answer generation service that emits required structured sections, truth labels, and trust value.
acceptance_focus: Assistant output consistently returns required sections and truth-label categories for bounded prompts.
Trust value is included and formatted for Telegram rendering contract.
slices.notes: Separates answer-shape generation from retrieval guardrails.

slices.description: Implement bounded retrieval/context assembly guardrails for assistant answers using active tracked-person read models only.
acceptance_focus: Assistant context assembly is bounded to active tracked-person scope and remains MCP-independent.
Read-model usage is auditable by scope/person/session and rejects cross-scope leakage.
slices.notes: Dedicated verification path for retrieval scoping and auditability.

slices.description: Implement Telegram assistant mode UI over active tracked-person context with Open in Web handoff.
acceptance_focus: Telegram assistant mode uses required response structure and labels.
Deep-analysis handoff uses bounded web links in active person context.
slices.notes: P1 assistant mode slice.

slices.description: Implement operator-layer offline-event record model, migration, and persistence boundary (separate from legacy Stage6 offline path).
acceptance_focus: Offline-event data contract is scoped to operator-layer requirements.
Persistence boundary avoids reopening legacy Stage6 command path semantics.
slices.notes: Backend-only storage boundary checkpoint, independent from web P0 completion.

slices.description: Implement offline-event operator read model/query path with tracked-person scope and timeline-linkage metadata.
acceptance_focus: Saved offline events are queryable by operator scope and tracked person with stable identifiers.
Read model exposes timeline-linkage/status metadata required by Telegram/web refinement flows.
slices.notes: Creates explicit queryable increment requested by parent acceptance.

slices.description: Implement Telegram offline-event capture intake (summary + optional recording reference) with draft persistence.
acceptance_focus: Telegram flow captures summary and optional recording reference in active tracked-person context.
Captured input persists as bounded offline-event draft with operator/session linkage.
slices.notes: Capture-input step isolated from clarification loop policy and refinement UI.

slices.description: Implement prioritized offline-event clarification-question loop with PRD stop-rule enforcement.
acceptance_focus: Clarification questions are ranked by expected information gain for the active offline-event draft.
Loop terminates on low-gain/repetition/unknown-answer patterns and supports partial-confidence save.
slices.notes: Narrow decision/policy slice for clarification ranking and stop conditions.

slices.description: Implement Telegram orchestration for offline-event clarification loop and partial-confidence final save.
acceptance_focus: Telegram operator can progress through clarification questions and save when stop conditions are reached.
Final saved offline event includes confidence/trust indicators and full clarification history linkage.
slices.notes: Completes bounded Telegram flow after capture and ranking policies are in place.

slices.description: Implement web offline-event inspection/refinement page with confidence/trust and clarification history.
acceptance_focus: Web exposes offline-event details needed for review/correction with confidence/trust and clarification history visibility.
Refinement actions remain bounded to the selected offline event and operator session.
slices.notes: Web refinement separated from timeline linkage mutation.

slices.description: Implement bounded timeline-linkage updates for refined offline events.
acceptance_focus: Operator-approved refinement can update offline-event timeline linkage in bounded scope.
Linkage updates are auditable and do not mutate unrelated person/timeline objects.
slices.notes: Dedicated write path for timeline-linkage mutations.

slices.description: Implement web persons list with search, unresolved badges, and recent-update indicators for tracked-person navigation.
acceptance_focus: Persons list supports entry into active person workspace.
List badges/signals reflect current resolution/unresolved state.
slices.notes: Start of P1 workspace expansion.

slices.description: Implement workspace shell and Summary section with trust/uncertainty/provenance visibility.
acceptance_focus: Workspace shell is stable for person-scoped navigation and section switching.
Summary section is grounded in durable read models with explicit trust/uncertainty signals.
slices.notes: Shell + Summary is a single bounded outcome and checkpoint.

slices.description: Implement Dossier section in person workspace with provenance visibility.
acceptance_focus: Dossier section is readable from durable read models in person-scoped workspace context.
Dossier facts expose provenance/trust markers and bounded drilldown entry points.
slices.notes: Dossier separated from Profile and timeline-heavy sections.

slices.description: Implement Profile section in person workspace with uncertainty and confidence signals.
acceptance_focus: Profile section is grounded in durable read models for the selected tracked person.
Confidence/uncertainty signals are explicit and navigable to supporting context.
slices.notes: Profile isolated from Pair/Timeline/Evidence/Revisions scopes.

slices.description: Implement Pair Dynamics section with bounded summary and evidence drilldown links.
acceptance_focus: Pair Dynamics view exposes current pair state and direction-of-change signals for the tracked person scope.
Pair-level drilldown links route to bounded evidence context.
slices.notes: Separates Pair Dynamics from Timeline rendering complexity.

slices.description: Implement Timeline section with bounded summary and revision/evidence drilldown.
acceptance_focus: Timeline view exposes key shifts in bounded tracked-person scope with recency ordering.
Timeline drilldown links to evidence/revision context without leaving bounded workspace.
slices.notes: Timeline isolated for clearer verification and recovery checkpoints.

slices.description: Implement Evidence section with durable-object evidence drilldown and provenance context.
acceptance_focus: Evidence section explains why durable objects exist with bounded provenance and confidence context.
Evidence navigation keeps person workspace scope stable.
slices.notes: Evidence-focused checkpoint separated from revision history.

slices.description: Implement Revisions section with durable-object change history and trigger context.
acceptance_focus: Revision history shows version/change cues and trigger context for bounded durable objects.
Operator can traverse revision history without leaving person workspace scope.
slices.notes: Revision-history checkpoint separated from evidence existence checks.

slices.description: Implement Resolution section/drilldown in person workspace.
acceptance_focus: Person workspace exposes Resolution section with unresolved/resolved status and bounded drilldown to resolution items.
Resolution drilldown reuses operator-layer contracts and preserves tracked-person context.
slices.notes: Explicit slice owner for parent requirement covering Resolution section.

slices.description: Define critical-alert taxonomy and default suppression policy across runtime defects, blocking resolution items, materialization failures, and control-plane stops.
acceptance_focus: Default alert policy is workflow-critical only and explicitly suppresses non-critical state churn.
Alert source classes, suppression rules, and escalation boundaries are explicit and testable.
slices.notes: P2 policy foundation before delivery surfaces.

slices.description: Implement Telegram critical-alert delivery and acknowledgement flow with bounded deep-link handoff into resolution context.
acceptance_focus: Only critical workflow blockers are pushed by default.
Acknowledgement and handoff preserve scope/person context.
slices.notes: Telegram alert surface.

slices.description: Implement web alerts list/grouping/filtering with links to related resolution/person objects.
acceptance_focus: Web alerts list is grouped/filterable and maps each alert to related resolution/person context.
Alert list remains workflow-focused and excludes non-critical default feed noise.
slices.notes: Core web alerts outcome separated from control-plane analytics widgets.

slices.description: Implement bounded web control/analytics widgets linked to alert context.
acceptance_focus: Control/analytics widgets are bounded, auditable, and linked to actionable alert context.
Widgets do not expose raw admin/debug consoles as product UI.
slices.notes: Keeps higher-risk control/analytics scope independent from base alerts list.

slices.description: Run bounded alerts validation for delivery, suppression, acknowledgement, and deep-link recovery.
acceptance_focus: Validation artifact proves critical-only delivery, suppression behavior, and acknowledgment persistence.
Deep-link handoff/recovery paths are verified for Telegram and web alert surfaces.
slices.notes: Final P2 validation checkpoint.
